message:
Requirements:
  1. Hit the public Dog CEO API (https://dog.ceo/api/breeds/list/all) with
     ubuntu_exec or shell_exec to get the real list of breeds.
  2. Pick 6 breeds well suited to apartment living. For each, fetch ONE real
     image URL via https://dog.ceo/api/breed/<breed>/images/random
  3. Produce a single self-contained HTML file (inline CSS, no external JS,
     no <script> tags) saved to /tmp/dogs.html with:
       - a hero section with charity name "Solis Paws"
       - a 6-card grid: one card per breed, each showing the real image URL,
         breed name, and a 2-sentence apartment-friendly blurb
       - a fake "Donate" form (name, email, amount) — no submit handler
  4. After writing the file, run `wc -c /tmp/dogs.html` and `grep -c "<img"
     /tmp/dogs.html` to verify the file is non-trivial and has 6 image tags.
  5. Save the breed list you picked to persistent memory via memory_save so
     future sessions can recall it.
  6. Reply with the FULL HTML inline (in a single fenced ```html block) so I
     can save it to my machine and open it in a browser.

plan.task: Fetch the list of dog breeds from the Dog CEO API using ubuntu_exec or shell_exec and store the response for further processing
Select 6 apartment-friendly dog breeds from the fetched list and for each breed, fetch a random image URL from the Dog CEO API
Create a self-contained HTML file with inline CSS, containing a hero section, a 6-card grid with breed information and images, and a fake donate form, then save it to /tmp/dogs.html
Verify the generated HTML file by running wc -c and grep -c commands, then save the selected breed list to persistent memory using memory_save and return the full HTML content
